### Crumple Sheet (KQ-099) — `crumple-sheet`
Wad it up; smooth it out.

Dismiss crumples the sheet into a paper ball — a deterministic facet mesh converges over the fading content, shading by how far each triangle collapsed, and the wad sits there as a button until you smooth it back out with a settle wobble.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/crumple-sheet`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The sheet's content.
- `crumpled / defaultCrumpled / onCrumpleChange`: boolean / boolean / (crumpled) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled wad state.
- `dismissLabel / restoreLabel`: string / string — Chip and wad wording.
- `height`: number (default 220) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The wad is a real button — focus hops from the dismiss chip to the wad and back, and both states are announced.
- Under reduced motion the mesh never mounts; the sheet and wad swap with a fast fade.

### Lenticular Card (KQ-100) — `lenticular-card`
Two faces, one ridge at a time.

A ridged-lens card holding two faces — sweeping across it flips one vertical ridge at a time, the wave trailing your hand, with a sheen band riding the active ridge; a house slider gives the keyboard the same sweep.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/lenticular-card`

Props:
- `a / b`: ReactNode / ReactNode — The two full-size faces, sliced across the ridges.
- `ridges`: number (default 14) — Strip count, clamped 4-28.
- `onDominantChange`: (side: "a" | "b") => void — Fires once when the majority face crosses over.
- `height`: number (default 210) — Card height in px.

Notes:
- The slider is always rendered and is the accessible control — arrows sweep by five, Home and End jump, and pointer sweeps keep its value honest.
- Under reduced motion pointer tracking is off and the slider sweeps with instant flips.

### Vanish Type (KQ-101) — `vanish-type`
One word in the slot at a time.

A statement set along a ray to its vanishing point — scroll pulls each word into the reading slot in turn while the rest recede up the ray or drift off behind, every position scrubbed 1:1 from scroll.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/vanish-type`

Props:
- `words`: string[] — Four to ten words of one statement.
- `onWordChange`: (index) => void — Fires as the reading slot changes words.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- The full statement lives in an sr-only paragraph — the visual words are presentational, and the region scrolls natively from the keyboard.
- Under reduced motion it renders as a plain wrapped headline with the active word underlined.

### Extrude Title (KQ-102) — `extrude-title`
Type with a block behind it.

A headline extruded in layered shadow — the block tilts with the pointer while the extrusion counter-rotates so the light reads as fixed, and pressing crushes the depth flat before it pops back on the recoil spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/extrude-title`

Props:
- `children`: string — The title text — a string, it is layered.
- `depth / stepPx`: number / number (default 8 / 1.1) — Extrusion steps and offset per step.
- `onPress`: () => void — Fires on the flattening press.

Notes:
- The title is a real button — Space and Enter flatten it exactly like a pointer press.
- Under reduced motion the extrusion is static and presses flatten with fast tweens instead of springs.

### Orbit Tags (KQ-103) — `orbit-tags`
A sphere of chips, one in reach.

A selectable tag sphere — chips ride a golden-angle lattice you can spin with momentum, arrows walk the roving tab stop while the sphere rotates each tag to the front, and selection glides its chip to face you.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/orbit-tags`

Props:
- `tags`: { id, label }[] — Up to sixteen chips on the lattice.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled single selection.
- `radius / height`: number / number (default 92 / 240) — Sphere radius and stage height.

Notes:
- Every chip is a real button on a roving tab stop; the ambient spin pauses under keyboard focus, hover, and offscreen.
- Under reduced motion the sphere renders as a flat chip cloud with the same selection and keyboard.

### Path Type (KQ-104) — `path-type`
Type that rides its own wave.

A sentence set on an undulating ribbon — scroll draws the path in while the type rides its wave, cresting and dipping with a leading tick, every position scrubbed 1:1 from scroll.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/path-type`

Props:
- `text`: string — One sentence to set on the ribbon.
- `amplitude / height`: number / number (default 26 / 260) — Wave height and scroll stage height.
- `onProgress`: (progress) => void — Streams the ride, deduped to hundredths.

Notes:
- The sentence lives in an sr-only paragraph; the SVG is presentational and the region scrolls natively from the keyboard.
- Under reduced motion the ribbon renders fully drawn with the type at rest.

### Billboard Run (KQ-105) — `billboard-run`
Copy you drive past.

Headlines on angled roadside billboards — scroll drives past them down a converging road, each board growing from the vanishing point, banking by the camera, and sweeping off the frame, all scrubbed 1:1.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/billboard-run`

Props:
- `boards`: { id, headline, deck? }[] — Three to six billboards along the road.
- `onPass`: (index) => void — Fires as each board sweeps past the camera.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only ordered list carries every headline; the road and boards are presentational and the region scrolls natively.
- Under reduced motion the boards render as a flat stacked list with the nearest one accented.

### Turn Word (KQ-106) — `turn-word`
Only the letters that differ turn.

One word becomes another letter by letter — cells half-turn edge-on, swap their glyph, and land on the snap spring in a cascade, while letters the two words share hold perfectly still.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/turn-word`

Props:
- `words`: [string, string] — The two states; the shorter is padded with figure spaces.
- `active / defaultActive / onTurn`: 0 | 1 / 0 | 1 / (active) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled facing word.
- `interactive`: boolean (default true) — Whether the word itself is a switch button.

Notes:
- Interactive mode is a real role=switch — Space and Enter turn it, and the settled word is announced.
- Under reduced motion the words crossfade whole with no letter turns.

### Converge Quote (KQ-107) — `converge-quote`
Lines that land in their own time.

A pull-quote scattered through depth — each line floats at its own distance and jitter until scroll assembles them onto one readable plane in staggered bands, the attribution arriving last under its rule.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/converge-quote`

Props:
- `lines`: string[] — Two to five lines of one quote.
- `attribution`: string — Arrives after the last line lands.
- `onConverge`: (converged) => void — Fires crossing 85% assembly, both directions.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only blockquote carries the full quote; the floating lines are presentational and the region scrolls natively.
- Under reduced motion the quote renders assembled and static.

### Shadow Script (KQ-108) — `shadow-script`
The shadow has something to say.

Drag the lamp along its arc and the type's cast shadow stretches, skews, and finally becomes legible — because the shadow is a second message, sharpening as the light swings low.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/shadow-script`

Props:
- `text / secret`: string / string — The standing headline and the message hidden in its shadow.
- `defaultAngle`: number (default 0) — Light angle, -60 to 60 degrees.
- `onReveal`: (revealed) => void — Fires crossing legibility in either direction.

Notes:
- The lamp is a real slider — arrows nudge by four degrees, Home and End reach the extremes, and aria-valuetext reads the angle.
- Both texts always live in an sr-only line; under reduced motion the shadow renders pre-stretched and legible.

### Punch Type (KQ-109) — `punch-type`
Letters as windows.

A word die-cut through the surface — the letterforms are true SVG mask cutouts onto a vista behind, and sweeping the pointer shifts the scene so the windows fill with moving color.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/punch-type`

Props:
- `text`: string — One short word, punched at display size.
- `scene`: ReactNode — The layer behind; defaults to a deterministic vista.
- `depth / height`: number / number (default 16 / 200) — Max scene counter-shift and stage height.

Notes:
- The plate is a labelled image with an adjacent interaction note; nothing inside is focusable.
- Under reduced motion and coarse pointers the vista rests at a fixed offset.

### Helix Index (KQ-110) — `helix-index`
Wind the spiral; read the front.

An index wound on a spiral staircase — winding the coil also travels it, so the front record always reads mid-stage; drag, scroll a notch, or arrow through, and Enter pulls the record facing you.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/helix-index`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, hint? }[] — Five to twelve records on the coil.
- `value / defaultValue / onSelect`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled selection; default also seats the coil.
- `radius / pitch / stepDeg / height`: number / number / number / number (default 96 / 34 / 40 / 260) — Coil geometry and stage height.

Notes:
- One roving tab stop — arrows wind one record, Home and End glide the coil, and the settled record is announced.
- Under reduced motion it renders as a flat list with the same buttons and selection.

### Ring Dial (KQ-111) — `ring-dial`
Stops that pull like magnets.

A lens ring in perspective — a knurled band whose detents pull the value in like magnets as you cross them; the fixed needle blinks on every land and the hub pulses the committed stop.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ring-dial`

Props:
- `min / max / detents / step`: number / number / number[] / number (default 0 / 100 / — / 10) — Range and stops; explicit detents win over the step grid.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange / onInput`: number / number / (value) => void / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled value; change fires per land, input streams live.
- `format`: (value) => string — Formats the hub readout, band numerals, and aria-valuetext.
- `size`: number (default 220) — Dial diameter in px.

Notes:
- A real slider — arrows step between detents, Home and End reach the first and last, and aria-valuetext speaks the formatted stop.
- Under reduced motion the ring renders flat and tracks the pointer one-to-one with no magnetic pull; the needle blink remains as the land cue.

### Z Accordion (KQ-112) — `z-accordion`
Sections that step back, not down.

An accordion that spends depth instead of height — the open sheet glides to the front while the rest step back into the rack, scaled, dimmed, and stacked so every header stays in reach.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/z-accordion`

Props:
- `items`: { id, title, content }[] — The sheets; three to five read best.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled open sheet — exactly one at a time.

Notes:
- True accordion semantics — header buttons carry aria-expanded, panels are labelled regions, and arrows, Home, and End move between headers.
- Under reduced motion it renders as a flat accordion in normal flow with instant expand and no recession.

### Depth Menu (KQ-113) — `depth-menu`
Branches recede; the trail hangs behind.

An inline menu that spends depth on hierarchy — entering a branch pushes the current level back into a hanging trail, a breadcrumb rail floats above for multi-level jumps, and focus always returns to the row you came from.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/depth-menu`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, hint?, children? }[] — The tree; branch nodes carry children.
- `onSelect`: (id, path) => void — Fires on a leaf with its ancestor path.
- `depthGap`: number (default 80) — Perspective-honest recession per level.

Notes:
- WAI-ARIA menu keyboard — arrows move, Right or Enter descends, Left or Escape pops with focus returned, and level changes are announced.
- Under reduced motion levels swap instantly and the breadcrumb alone carries the context.

### Stage Tabs (KQ-114) — `stage-tabs`
Panels on a revolve.

Tabs staged like a theater revolve — the outgoing panel sweeps to one wing as the incoming one revolves in from the other, direction following tab order, over a floor shadow that squashes with the turn.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/stage-tabs`

Props:
- `tabs`: { id, label, content }[] — The tab set.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active tab.
- `minHeight`: number (default 180) — Stage floor height in px.

Notes:
- Real tabs — roving tabindex, arrows auto-activate with wrap, Home and End jump, and the panel itself is focusable.
- Under reduced motion panels crossfade instantly and the underline jumps without gliding.

### Card Fan (KQ-115) — `card-fan`
Fan, sweep, play.

A hand of cards that fans open around a shared pivot — sweep to raise one, siblings part around it, and picking flies the card to a played slot where it lands on the recoil spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/card-fan`

Props:
- `cards`: { id, label, content }[] — Up to seven cards in the hand.
- `value / defaultValue / onSelect`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled played card.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled fan state.
- `cardWidth`: number (default 120) — Card width in px; height is 1.45×.

Notes:
- The open hand is a radiogroup — arrows move the raised card, Enter or Space plays it, Escape folds the hand and returns focus to the stack.
- Under reduced motion the fan opens flat and instantly, raising is a highlight, and picks move without flight or bump.

### Hallway Menu (KQ-116) — `hallway-menu`
Every stop looks down its own hallway.

A mega-menu of parallel hallways — sweeping the rail looks down each corridor in one-point perspective, doors hinge off the walls toward a far-wall plate, and the whole view swings sideways between stops.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hallway-menu`

Props:
- `hallways`: { id, label, items: { id, label, hint? }[] }[] — Two to five hallways on the rail.
- `onSelect`: (hallwayId, itemId) => void — Fires when a door is chosen.
- `corridorHeight`: number (default 220) — Corridor stage height in px.

Notes:
- Rail buttons carry aria-expanded; ArrowDown enters the corridor, arrows walk the doors in documented wall order, and Escape returns to the rail.
- Under reduced motion each corridor renders as a flat two-column panel with instant open and the same keyboard.

### Deck Switcher (KQ-117) — `deck-switcher`
Views raked back, one zooms forward.

An app-switcher for views — open the deck and every view rakes back into a tilted stack; slide the rake by drag, wheel, or arrows, then commit and the chosen view zooms forward while the rest fold away.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/deck-switcher`

Props:
- `views`: { id, title, content }[] — Two to six views; all stay mounted so their state survives switches.
- `activeId / defaultActiveId / onActiveChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active view; fires only when the commit changes it.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The open deck is a listbox — arrows slide the rake, Home and End jump, Enter commits, and Escape cancels back to the previous view with focus returned.
- Under reduced motion the deck is a flat list of title cards and commits swap instantly.

### Flyover Map (KQ-118) — `flyover-map`
The page, miniature and tilted.

A scroll region with its own tilted minimap — districts sized to their sections, a live viewport indicator riding the scroll, and a click that flies the view to any district on the glide spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flyover-map`

Props:
- `sections`: { id, label, content }[] — Three to six sections stacked in the scroll region.
- `onArrive`: (id) => void — Fires when a flight lands or scrolling settles in a new section.
- `height`: number (default 320) — Viewport height in px.

Notes:
- The minimap is a navigation landmark of real buttons — aria-current tracks the active district and arrivals are announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the map renders flat and district clicks jump instantly with no flight.

### Gate Stepper (KQ-119) — `gate-stepper`
The gates ahead stay in sight.

A wizard where progress is distance — every remaining gate is visible ahead, receding through the current one; advancing sweeps the active gate past the camera while the line moves up a slot, and a refused gate shakes its head.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gate-stepper`

Props:
- `steps`: { id, title, content }[] — Two to five gates.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled position.
- `canAdvance`: (index) => boolean — Gate check before Next; a refusal shakes and announces.
- `nextLabel / backLabel / doneLabel / onComplete`: string / string / string / () => void — Footer wording and the finish callback.

Notes:
- The active gate is a labelled group and every change or refusal is announced politely; ahead and passed gates are hidden from AT.
- Under reduced motion it is a flat stepper — no recession, instant swaps, refusals announce without the shake.

### Hinge Nav (KQ-120) — `hinge-nav`
Doors on the frame, not drawers in it.

Navigation doors hinged on the frame edges — pull a side tab and the panel swings in from edge-on over the dimmed stage, bumping the frame as it lands; Escape or the stage swings it shut.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hinge-nav`

Props:
- `doors`: { side: "left" | "right", label, items }[] — One door per side; one open at a time.
- `children`: ReactNode — The stage content the doors swing over.
- `onSelect`: (side, id) => void — Fires when a door row is chosen; the door closes after.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Tabs carry aria-expanded, the door is a labelled region, focus moves to the first row on open and returns to the tab on Escape.
- Under reduced motion doors fade-slide in flat with no rotation or frame bump.

### Pop Book (KQ-121) — `pop-book`
Cutouts that stand up from the fold.

A pop-up spread that opens with scroll — the two page halves relax apart while hinged cutouts stand up from the center fold rank by rank, back row first, every rise scrubbed 1:1.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pop-book`

Props:
- `pieces`: { id, node, row? }[] — Three to six cutouts; row 0 is the back rank.
- `onOpenChange`: (openness) => void — Streams the spread, deduped to twentieths.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only list carries every cutout; the spread is presentational and the region scrolls natively.
- Under reduced motion the plate renders fully open and static.

### Iso Blocks (KQ-122) — `iso-blocks`
A district that rises to meet you.

An isometric block city drawn entirely in flat 2D — every cuboid is three clip-path faces shaded from one hue, so the projection is Safari-proof by construction; blocks rise to the hand and light their roofs.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iso-blocks`

Props:
- `blocks`: { id, label, storeys?, hue? }[] — Four to twelve blocks on a near-square grid.
- `onInspect`: (id) => void — Fires when a block is pressed.
- `cell`: number (default 64) — Grid cell width in px.

Notes:
- Every block is a real button — keyboard focus raises and lights it exactly like hover.
- Under reduced motion the city reads as a flat top-down survey with the same buttons.

### Lift Tray (KQ-123) — `lift-tray`
Every tool floats to your hand.

Tools that float up to your hand — each plate levitates off the tray with a contact shadow that shrinks and softens as it rises, neighbors lifting in sympathy, item shadow and ground ellipse two views of one altitude.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/lift-tray`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, node }[] — Three to five tools on the tray.
- `onPick`: (id) => void — Fires when a tool is pressed.
- `maxLift`: number (default 14) — Peak levitation in px.

Notes:
- Every tool is a real button — keyboard focus levitates it exactly like hover, and picks are announced.
- Under reduced motion hover is a flat highlight and the ground shadows rest static.

### Shaker Dome (KQ-124) — `shaker-dome`
The flakes take the long way down.

A keepsake dome over a diorama — shake it by hand and the seeded flakes swirl off the true semicircular shell, settling on gravity until the scene stands still and the loop stands down.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/shaker-dome`

Props:
- `scene`: ReactNode — The diorama under the glass; defaults to a minted monument.
- `flakes`: number (default 42) — Particle count, capped at eighty.
- `label / height`: ReactNode / number (default nameplate / 240) — Plinth engraving and stage height.

Notes:
- The STIR button gives the keyboard a standard kick and stirs are announced; the sim idles the moment the flakes rest.
- The loop pauses offscreen and in hidden tabs; under reduced motion one settled frame renders and the shake is disabled.

### Daylight Dial (KQ-125) — `daylight-dial`
Drag the sky; the shadows obey.

A time-of-day dial over a skyline — one hour value drives the sky's keyframed color ramp, the sun and moon in anti-phase on their arc, sweeping building shadows with honest projection, and stars that keep their hours.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/daylight-dial`

Props:
- `hour / defaultHour / onHourChange`: number / number / (hour) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled 0-24 clock; settles announce integers.
- `height`: number (default 250) — Scene height in px.

Notes:
- The track is one slider — arrows step an hour, Page keys three, Home and End bound the day, and aria-valuetext reads the clock.
- Naturally reduced-motion-safe: the scene scrubs one-to-one with the dial and merely loses its spring lag.

### Swing Door (KQ-151) — `swing-door`
Past the detent or slammed home.

A hinged door with handle physics — drag it past the detent and it swings wide on the glide spring; let go early and it slams home, shuddering the frame and puffing a dust line at the jamb.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/swing-door`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — What waits in the doorway.
- `doorFace`: ReactNode — Artwork on the door; defaults to a minted panel.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled door state.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Frame height in px.

Notes:
- The handle is a real button with aria-expanded; Escape slams the door and focus returns to the handle.
- Under reduced motion the door slides flat with no slam or shudder.

### Hatch Board (KQ-152) — `hatch-board`
Every lid rides its own spring.

A grid of spring-loaded hatches — every lid pops open on its own snap spring to reveal the well beneath, latches shut with a click pulse, and the whole board can cascade open or closed from a corner.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hatch-board`

Props:
- `hatches`: { id, label, lid, well }[] — Four to nine hatches; the label names each disclosure.
- `openIds / defaultOpenIds / onOpenChange`: string[] / string[] / (openIds) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled open set.
- `columns / cellAspect`: number / number (default 3 / 1.1) — Board shape.

Notes:
- Each lid is a real disclosure button; wells are labelled regions, inert while shut, and the open count is announced.
- Under reduced motion lids fade flat with no pops or cascade.

### Gear Train (KQ-153) — `gear-train`
Ratios you can feel.

Three meshed gears with honest ratios — drag any wheel and the transmission carries your hand to a needle on the output shaft; teeth stay interleaved at every angle by construction, and releases coast on a velocity-seeded drift.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gear-train`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number / number / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled needle value, 0-100; fires once per settle.
- `teeth`: [number, number, number] (default [12, 18, 24]) — Drive, idler, and output tooth counts.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The train is one slider — arrows step by two, Page keys by ten, Home and End glide the rails, and every settle is announced by value.
- Under reduced motion gears track the hand one-to-one with no coast; the tooth-tick blink remains as an opacity cue.

### Commit Lever (KQ-154) — `commit-lever`
Past the guard, then all the way.

A two-stage action lever — the pull stiffens at the guard notch and the flap swings aside as you push through to arm; full travel commits with a thunk, locks, and glides home after the reset.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/commit-lever`

Props:
- `onCommit`: () => void — Required — fires exactly once per full travel.
- `onArmChange`: (armed) => void — Tracks the armed stage, deduped.
- `commitLabel / resetDelay / disabled`: string / number / boolean (default "Commit" / 900 / false) — Wording, auto-return delay (0 holds), and the safety.

Notes:
- The grip is a real button — first press arms, second commits, Escape disarms; every stage is announced.
- Under reduced motion the lever snaps between its three stations with no thunk or lamp blink.

### Crank Reel (KQ-155) — `crank-reel`
Wind forward; the freewheel only clicks.

A hand-crank that winds progress onto a visible spool — forward turns bank units with ratchet ticks while backward cranking just chatters on the freewheel; the drum trails the handle on the glide spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/crank-reel`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number / number / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled wound units; fires per unit.
- `max / unitsPerTurn / reversible`: number / number / boolean (default 12 / 2 / false) — Capacity, gearing, and whether backward unwinds.
- `onSlip / spoolLabel`: () => void / string — Freewheel slip callback and the wound material's name.

Notes:
- The rig is one slider — arrows wind a unit, Home and End run the rails with a single batched announcement, and slips announce as freewheel.
- Under reduced motion the crank and drum move instantly and the pawl kick becomes an opacity blink.

### Pulley Lift (KQ-156) — `pulley-lift`
The counterweight lands the car.

A dumbwaiter on one honest travel value — haul the platform, the counterweight, or either rope fall, and the car settles to the nearest floor with an elastic arrival while the sheave turns by exactly the rope it passed.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pulley-lift`

Props:
- `floors`: { id, label, content }[] — Two to four floors, ground first.
- `floor / defaultFloor / onFloorChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled floor; fires on settle.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Shaft height in px.

Notes:
- The frame is a vertical slider and every floor tab is a real button with aria-current; arrivals are announced by label.
- Under reduced motion the car jumps between floors with no glide or bounce.

### Combo Dials (KQ-157) — `combo-dials`
Three dials, one agreement.

A combination lock of stacked cylinder dials — digits sweep the rim under your drag, settle into detents with a click, and when every dial agrees with the setting the bolt slides open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/combo-dials`

Props:
- `length`: number (default 3) — Dial count, two to five.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled digits; fires per settled digit.
- `secret / onUnlock`: string / () => void — The setting to match and the single-fire unlock callback.

Notes:
- Each dial is a spinbutton — arrows step with wrap, typing a digit glides straight to it, and settles are announced per dial.
- Under reduced motion digits jump detent by detent and the bolt opens with a fast fade; near-misses never announce proximity.

### Zipper Seam (KQ-158) — `zipper-seam`
The seam parts above the pull.

Fabric halves joined by a toothed seam — draw the pull down and they part in a V above it, teeth shearing aside along the edges, with a shiver when the yank lands fully open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/zipper-seam`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — What lies in the seam.
- `progress / defaultProgress / onProgressChange`: number / number / (progress) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled 0-100 opening; fires on settle.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The pull is a vertical slider — arrows step by five, Page keys by twenty, Home and End seal and open; ends snap and announce.
- Under reduced motion the seam tracks the hand one-to-one with no snaps or shiver.

### Trapdoor Drop (KQ-159) — `trapdoor-drop`
The long way down, on purpose.

A two-stage disposal platform — arming slides the bolts aside while the item wobbles, confirming swings both leaves down from their outer hinges, and the item accelerates into the well past blinking depth rings before the doors clap shut.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/trapdoor-drop`

Props:
- `children / itemKey`: ReactNode / string — The item on the doors; change the key to lower in the next one.
- `onDrop`: () => void — Fires exactly once when the item has fallen through.
- `armLabel / dropLabel / disarmLabel`: string / string / string — The two-stage wording.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Dismissal is always two presses — arm, then confirm — with Escape backing out; every stage is announced.
- Under reduced motion the item fades instantly on confirm with the same two-stage semantics.

### Drawbridge (KQ-160) — `drawbridge`
Chains out, deck down, crossing proved.

A span between two content islands — lowering pays the chains out from the gantry as the deck swings down, thumps the far abutment on the recoil, and a runner crosses once to prove the connection before the far bank wakes.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/drawbridge`

Props:
- `near / far`: ReactNode / ReactNode — The two banks; the far one is reachable only while bridged.
- `bridged / defaultBridged / onBridgedChange`: boolean / boolean / (bridged) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled span state.
- `lowerLabel / raiseLabel`: string / string — Toggle wording.
- `height`: number (default 250) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- One real toggle with aria-expanded; the far bank is inert and dimmed until the deck lands, and both states are announced.
- Under reduced motion the deck fades between raised and bridged with no chains or runner.

### Command Deck (KB-101) — `command-deck`
A palette that arms before it fires.

A command palette that arms before it fires — a deliberate fill sweeps the selected row before execution, so destructive commands never feel accidental.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/command-deck`

Props:
- `commands`: DeckCommand[] — { id, label, hint?, icon?, section?, keywords?, destructive?, run } per command.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `hotkey`: boolean (default true) — Global ⌘K / Ctrl+K toggle listener.
- `inline / portal`: boolean (default false / true) — Inline renders just the panel (docs/demos); portal overlays into body with focus trap and scroll lock.
- `recentKey`: string (default "kinetiq-deck-recents") — localStorage key for the five most recent command ids.

Notes:
- Full combobox semantics with focus trap and restore; Escape mid-arm cancels the sweep.
- Under reduced motion the arming sweep becomes an instant fill with a deliberate 120ms pause.

### Access Panel (KB-102) — `access-panel`
Sign-in that unlocks like a vault.

Sign-in that unlocks like a vault — steps ride a gantry rail, OTP digits drop into their cells, and the right code throws a breaker bolt open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/access-panel`

Props:
- `expectedCode`: string (default "246810") — The six-digit code that throws the bolt.
- `onVerify`: (code: string) => void — Fires with the entered code on every attempt.
- `onComplete`: (email: string) => void — Fires once when the correct code lands.
- `email`: string — Prefills the email field.

Notes:
- One hidden input with autocomplete="one-time-code" drives the six visual cells — the accessibility-correct OTP pattern.
- Under reduced motion the rail slides become fades and the rejection nudge is skipped; the alert still announces.

### Signal Center (KB-103) — `signal-center`
An inbox that files itself.

An inbox that files itself — signals arrive on the conveyor, swipe away with real velocity, and clear-all sweeps the floor in a cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/signal-center`

Props:
- `signals / defaultSignals / onSignalsChange`: Signal[] / Signal[] / (signals) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled feed; Signal = { id, source, title, detail?, time, read? }.
- `onArchive`: (signal: Signal) => void — Fires per archived signal, including clear-all.
- `title`: string (default "Signals") — Header title next to the live unread readout.

Notes:
- Archiving is keyboard-reachable via a per-row button; swiping is the fast path, not the only path.
- Under reduced motion arrivals and exits become fades and clear-all loses its stagger.

### Media Console (KB-104) — `media-console`
A media island that unfolds into a console.

A media island that unfolds into a console — the pill morphs open into transport, a caliper timeline, and a scope-scrubbed volume.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/media-console`

Props:
- `tracks`: Track[] — { id, title, artist, duration } — playback is simulated; the JSDoc shows wiring a real audio element.
- `defaultExpanded`: boolean (default false) — Start as the pill or the full console.

Notes:
- Complete keyboard transport; Escape collapses the console back to the pill.
- Under reduced motion the morph becomes a crossfade and the waveform holds still.

### Checkout Receipt (KB-105) — `checkout-receipt`
Payment that prints its proof.

Payment that prints its proof — hold to pay, then a receipt feeds out of the slot line by line, tears off, and takes a PAID stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/checkout-receipt`

Props:
- `items`: ReceiptItem[] — Line items ({ name, price }) summed into the total.
- `currency`: string (default "$") — Price prefix.
- `onPay`: () => void — Fires when the hold completes.

Notes:
- The receipt is real text in a definition list; only the barcode is decorative.
- Under reduced motion the receipt appears whole and the stamp fades in.

### Intake Tray (KB-106) — `intake-tray`
Files land, physically.

Files land, physically — dropped files fall into the tray with a squash, shingle into a stack, fill their gauge rings, and slide to the processed rail.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/intake-tray`

Props:
- `onFiles`: (files: File[]) => void — Receives each accepted batch.
- `accept / maxFiles`: string / number (default — / 6) — Accepted types hint and tray capacity.
- `simulate`: boolean (default true) — Hash-driven demo progress; the JSDoc documents wiring real uploads.
- `ref`: Ref<IntakeTrayHandle> — Imperative addFiles() for programmatic intake.

Notes:
- The drop zone is a real button with a labeled native file input — fully keyboard operable.
- Under reduced motion chips stack neatly with fades; the gauge rings still fill.

### Readout Grid (KB-107) — `readout-grid`
Dashboard stats as instrument cards.

Dashboard stats as instrument cards — a carry-rolling counter, a self-drawing sparkline, a split-flap delta, and a needle gauge, mounted with one cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/readout-grid`

Props:
- `metrics`: ReadoutGridMetrics — Optional full override of the four cards: counter, sparkline, flap, and gauge shapes.

Notes:
- Each card is an article whose label reads naturally to screen readers; chart visuals carry sr-only values.
- Under reduced motion cards render in place, the sparkline arrives fully drawn, and the needle jumps.

### Launch Checklist (KB-108) — `launch-checklist`
Setup steps that stamp themselves done.

Onboarding checklist where ticks draw themselves, finished steps strike through and settle to the bottom, and completing the set lands a CALIBRATED stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/launch-checklist`

Props:
- `steps`: ChecklistStep[] — Step definitions: id, title, optional description.
- `completed / defaultCompleted`: string[] — Controlled or uncontrolled set of completed step ids.
- `onCompletedChange`: (completed: string[]) => void — Fires on every toggle with the new completed set.
- `onComplete`: () => void — Fires once when the final step completes.

Notes:
- Rows are native checkboxes under the hood — the whole block is keyboard and screen-reader operable.
- Under reduced motion rows reorder instantly and the stamp fades in; the progress track still reports value via ARIA.

### Iris Menu (KB-109) — `iris-menu`
Actions bloom from where you pressed.

Actions bloom from where you pressed — items launch from the trigger's center to seats along the freest quadrant's arc, and fold back in on close.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iris-menu`

Props:
- `items`: IrisMenuItem[] — Up to six: { id, icon, label, onSelect, disabled?, destructive? }.
- `placement`: "auto" | quadrant (default "auto") — Auto measures the viewport and blooms into the roomiest quadrant.
- `radius`: number (default 84) — Distance from trigger center to each seat.

Notes:
- Arrow keys rotate focus around the ring; Escape closes and restores the trigger.
- Under reduced motion items fade in already seated — no travel.

### Overflow Rail (KB-110) — `overflow-rail`
More actions, sprung from the dots.

More actions, sprung from the dots — the pill rail morphs open inline while primaries yield with a recoil part and secondaries cascade in.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/overflow-rail`

Props:
- `primary / secondary`: RailAction[] — Always-visible actions and the set revealed by the ⋯ trigger.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled expansion.
- `label`: string (default "Actions") — Accessible toolbar name.

Notes:
- One toolbar: arrows rove across every visible action; Escape collapses and refocuses the trigger.
- Under reduced motion the width jumps and secondaries fade in place.

### Beacon (KB-111) — `beacon`
One capsule, every live activity.

One capsule, every live activity — the shell morphs between timer, upload, call, and now-playing views with blur crossfades, corners pinned.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/beacon`

Props:
- `activity`: BeaconActivity | null — Discriminated union: timer (endsAt), upload (progress), call (accept/decline), playing (title/artist); null collapses to a standby pill.
- `expanded / defaultExpanded / onExpandedChange`: boolean / boolean / (expanded) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled detail row.
- `onDismiss`: () => void — Renders a dismiss action in the detail row.

Notes:
- Call buttons are reachable while collapsed; activity changes announce once, debounced.
- Under reduced motion the shell resizes instantly and the loops hold still.

### Field Report (KB-112) — `field-report`
Feedback that files itself.

Feedback that files itself — a notch rating with a gliding indicator, a growing note field, and a submit that folds the form into a slot under a LOGGED stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/field-report`

Props:
- `onSubmit`: ({ rating, note }) => void — Fires on valid submit; rating is 1–5.
- `prompt / endLabels`: string / [string, string] (default "How did it feel?" / ["Rough","Dialed"]) — The question and the rail's end labels.
- `resetAfterMs`: number — Fades a fresh form back in after filing.

Notes:
- The rating is a real radiogroup — arrows move the indicator, and empty submits nudge with a role=alert line.
- Under reduced motion the filing collapse becomes a fade and the stamp lands statically.

### Not Found (KB-113) — `not-found`
Sweep complete. Sector empty.

Sweep complete, sector empty — a radar arc scans a quiet grid while the 404 numeral deciphers itself, with home and command-deck exits.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/not-found`

Props:
- `homeHref`: string (default "/") — Return-to-base link target.
- `onCommandDeck`: () => void — Renders the ghost command-deck action when provided.
- `code / message`: string (default "404" / sector message) — The cipher numeral and the rising subline.

Notes:
- The heading carries real text for screen readers; the cipher layer and radar are decorative.
- Under reduced motion the sweep freezes mid-arc and the numeral renders plainly.

### Forecast Card (KB-114) — `forecast-card`
Every vote moves every bar.

Every vote moves every bar — one commit renormalizes the whole field while percentages carry-roll and the leader tick migrates to the new front-runner.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/forecast-card`

Props:
- `question / options`: string / ForecastOption[] — 2–4 outcomes as { id, label, votes }; options are always the source of counts.
- `votedId / defaultVotedId / onVote`: string | null / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled viewer vote.
- `allowRevote`: boolean (default false) — Let the viewer move their vote between outcomes.
- `closesAt`: string | Date — Compact mono CLOSES footer line.

Notes:
- Rows are aria-pressed buttons; each vote announces the new percentage and leadership once.
- Under reduced motion widths jump and the leader tick teleports; the readouts pulse instead of rolling.

### Balance Card (KB-115) — `balance-card`
A balance with a private side.

A balance with a private side — the numeral carry-rolls behind a privacy blur, a sparkline draws itself, and the card flips to its activity face.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/balance-card`

Props:
- `balance / format / delta / series`: number / (v)=>string / {value,direction} / number[] — The carry-rolling amount, its delta chip, and the sparkline data.
- `activity`: BalanceActivity[] — Back-face rows: { id, label, amount, time }.
- `onAction`: (action: "send" | "receive" | "convert") => void — Fires from the action row.
- `defaultHidden`: boolean (default false) — Start with the balance blurred.

Notes:
- The hidden face is inert and aria-hidden — the flip never traps focus or leaks taps.
- Under reduced motion the flip is instant, the sparkline arrives drawn, and the blur toggles without a tween.

### Exchange Panel (KB-116) — `exchange-panel`
Two units, one clean swap.

Two units, one clean swap — the edited side is live, the computed side carry-rolls after a debounce, and swapping keeps focus while contents trade places.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/exchange-panel`

Props:
- `units`: ExchangeUnit[] | ExchangeGroup[] — Linear factors to a shared base unit; groups render a grouped select.
- `feeRate`: number (default 0) — Fraction applied to the converted side, shown as a fee row.
- `onChange / onDirectionChange`: (state) => void / (from, to) => void — Conversion updates and swap events.
- `debounceMs`: number (default 200) — Delay before the computed side rolls.

Notes:
- One side holds authority; the other is always derived — swapping transfers authority with the gesture and refocuses the input.
- Under reduced motion contents swap instantly and the readout uses its highlight fallback.

### Utils — `utils`
cn() — class composition.

Class-name composition helper shared by every component.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/utils`

### Calibration Set — `motion`
flick · snap · glide · drift · recoil.

Kinetiq's five calibrated springs plus the tween scale, cascade budget, and exit rules — the shared physics vocabulary every component draws from.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/motion`

### useMotionSafe — `use-motion-safe`
Reduced motion, decided once.

Single source of truth for reduced-motion decisions: combines the OS preference with an optional app-level override context.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/use-motion-safe`

### Spatial Set — `spatial`
Perspective, detents, orbits, lift.

The shared geometry vocabulary of the spatial instruments — house perspective range, angle detents, contact-lift shadows, orbit projection, and deterministic seeding.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spatial`

### usePointerTilt — `use-pointer-tilt`
Pointer → sprung perspective.

Normalized pointer tracking mapped to sprung tilt values — the house idiom for pointer-driven perspective, with an underdamped rebalance on leave and a fine-pointer gate.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/use-pointer-tilt`

### Agent Rules — `agents-rules`
The design-system operating rules for agents.

Kinetiq's operating rules for coding agents: the five-spring motion language, the tween scale, the 600ms cascade budget, the reduced-motion policy, token usage, and composition guidance. Installs as AGENTS.md at your repo root so your agent stays on the system's vocabulary.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/agents-rules`
